concepts.label: Force
concepts.definition: A push or pull on an object resulting from interaction with another object

concepts.label: Energy
concepts.definition: The capacity to do work or produce heat, existing in various forms such as kinetic, potential, thermal, and chemical

concepts.label: Mass
concepts.definition: A fundamental property of matter that determines its resistance to acceleration when a force is applied

concepts.label: Velocity
concepts.definition: The rate of change of position with respect to time, including both magnitude and direction

concepts.label: Acceleration
concepts.definition: The rate of change of velocity with respect to time, caused by the application of force

concepts.label: Gravity
concepts.definition: The fundamental force of attraction between objects with mass, responsible for planetary motion and weight

concepts.label: Momentum
concepts.definition: The product of an object's mass and velocity, a conserved quantity in isolated systems

concepts.label: Work
concepts.definition: Energy transferred to or from an object via the application of force along a displacement

concepts.label: Power
concepts.definition: The rate at which work is done or energy is transferred per unit of time

concepts.label: Pressure
concepts.definition: The force applied perpendicular to the surface of an object per unit area

concepts.label: Temperature
concepts.definition: A measure of the average kinetic energy of particles in a substance

concepts.label: Entropy
concepts.definition: A measure of the disorder or randomness in a system, related to the number of microscopic arrangements

concepts.label: Wave
concepts.definition: A disturbance that travels through space and time, transferring energy without transferring matter

concepts.label: Frequency
concepts.definition: The number of occurrences of a repeating event per unit of time, measured in hertz

concepts.label: Photon
concepts.definition: A quantum of electromagnetic radiation, the fundamental particle of light with zero rest mass

concepts.label: Electron
concepts.definition: A subatomic particle with negative electric charge, fundamental to atoms and electric current

concepts.label: Magnetism
concepts.definition: The physical phenomenon produced by the motion of electric charge, creating attractive or repulsive forces

concepts.label: Voltage
concepts.definition: The electric potential difference between two points, driving electric current through a circuit

concepts.label: Current
concepts.definition: The flow of electric charge through a conductor, measured in amperes

concepts.label: Resistance
concepts.definition: The opposition to the flow of electric current in a material, measured in ohms
